bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: mod should be path
the trait bound `Condition: JsonSchema` is not satisfied (also `Time: JsonSchema`)
bugs.root_cause: k8s-openapi 0.23 only implements JsonSchema for Condition/Time when its `schemars` feature is enabled; the workspace had only `v1_31`.
bugs.fix: Added `schemars` to k8s-openapi features in the workspace [workspace.dependencies] table.

bugs.error_message: use of unresolved crate `serde_yaml` in crates/banlieue-api/src/bin/crdgen.rs
bugs.root_cause: `crdgen` is a feature-gated binary that depends on serde_yaml, but serde_yaml was only in [dev-dependencies] so the binary could not see it.
bugs.fix: Added `serde_yaml = { workspace = true, optional = true }` to [dependencies] and made the `crdgen` feature pull it in via `crdgen = ["dep:serde_yaml"]`.

bugs.error_message: panic in kube-core schema flattener: Property "source" has the schema [enum: "static"] but was already defined as [enum: "dhcp", description: ...]
bugs.root_cause: kube-derive's CRD schema flattener is fundamentally incompatible with `#[serde(tag = "...")]` enums. By construction each variant subschema fixes the discriminator property to a *different* enum value (`dhcp`, `static`, `pool`), and kube-core's flattener demands an identical schema for any property used in multiple subschemas. The schemars 0.8 panic surfaced a description-metadata mismatch first; on schemars 1 + kube 3 the discriminator-value mismatch is what panics. Tagged enums cannot survive CRD generation either way. Stripping doc comments removed one symptom but not the cause.
bugs.fix: FIXED 2026-05-25 by restructuring `IpamSpec` from a serde-tagged enum into a flat struct: `IpamSpec { source: IpamSource, static: Option<StaticIpamConfig>, pool: Option<PoolIpamConfig> }`. New `IpamSource` is a plain `Dhcp|Static|Pool` enum that serializes as a lower-case string. Wire format changed (sub-config now nested under a sibling field named after the discriminator), which is Kubernetes-idiomatic and matches how CAPI structures similar shapes. Cross-field validation (`source=Static implies static is set`) is intentionally NOT schema-enforced — left to the controller and/or a future CEL rule. The three `*_currently_panics_due_to_ipam_spec_bug` tests were replaced with positive CRD-metadata assertions. Verified end-to-end: `cargo run -p banlieue-api --bin crdgen --features crdgen` now emits 6 valid CRDs.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.fix: Rewrote 29→37 lines (10 removed) | Also: /// IPAM configuration for a network interface. Th; /// Variants:
Changed to 't relax the principle. (Rationale is in the maint

bugs.error_message: error validating "deploy/crds/banlieue-crds.yaml": error validating data: failed to download openapi: Get "http://localhost:8080/openapi/v2?timeout=32s": dial tcp [::1]:8080: connect: connection refused
bugs.root_cause: `kubectl apply --dry-run=client` performs server-side OpenAPI validation by default: it tries to fetch the cluster's /openapi/v2 to validate the manifest. In a GitHub Actions runner there is no cluster and no kubeconfig, so kubectl falls back to localhost:8080 (the legacy default) and connection-refuses. `--dry-run=client` controls *application* mode, not *validation* mode — they are independent flags.
bugs.fix: Added `--validate=false` to skip the OpenAPI fetch (kubectl still structurally parses and shape-checks the manifest), then added a `grep -c '^kind: CustomResourceDefinition'` sanity check to assert the generator produced at least one CRD. Locally verifies: 6 CRDs generated and parsed.

bugs.error_message: Error: Resource not accessible by integration - https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflow-runs#get-a-workflow-run (in github/codeql-action/upload-sarif step of the SAST/Semgrep job)
bugs.root_cause: `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif` calls the GitHub REST API `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}` to gather workflow-run metadata used for SARIF fingerprinting (deduplication of repeat findings across runs). That endpoint requires the `actions:read` permission, which is NOT included in the default GITHUB_TOKEN scope and was not declared at the job level. The job had `security-events: write` (lets it upload SARIF) but not `actions: read` (lets it look up the workflow run).
bugs.fix: Added `actions: read` to the job-level `permissions:` block in sast.yaml (semgrep job), codeql.yaml (analyze job), and scorecard.yaml (analysis job — replaced the commented-out hint). All three workflows use upload-sarif and would hit the same failure on later runs.

bugs.error_message: unable to recognize "deploy/crds/banlieue-crds.yaml": Get "http://localhost:8080/api?timeout=32s": dial tcp [::1]:8080: connect: connection refused
bugs.root_cause: Sequel to bug-016. `kubectl apply --validate=false` skipped OpenAPI schema validation but kubectl still calls the cluster's `/api` discovery endpoint to *resolve* each kind/apiVersion to a REST resource (the "mapper" phase). Discovery has no offline flag in kubectl — `apply` cannot function without contacting an API server. `--dry-run=client` only controls whether the resolved operation is executed; it does not bypass discovery.
bugs.fix: Replaced the kubectl validation step entirely with an inline Python YAML validator: parse every document with yaml.safe_load_all, assert each has apiVersion=apiextensions.k8s.io/v1, kind=CustomResourceDefinition, a non-empty metadata.name, and at least one spec.versions[]. Removed the azure/setup-kubectl install step. Python 3 is preinstalled on ubuntu-latest, so no install. `kube-derive` already guarantees structural correctness at compile time, so the validator only needs to verify generator output shape — not enforce K8s-API-server semantics.
